title: Enhancement of charge correlations and real-space topological marker on an interacting non-Hermitian Su-Schrieffer-Heeger model
authors: Sebastião dos A. Sousa-Júnior, Pedro B. Melo, Rubem Mondaini, Arnob Kumar Ghosh, Rodrigo Arouca
abstract: We investigate the interacting non-Hermitian Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) model, focusing on the interplay between topology and charge ordering. Using a real-space topological marker, charge correlations, and the complex many-body spectrum, we map out the phase diagram under periodic and open boundary conditions. We show that the topological marker remains a robust diagnostic of non-Hermitian topological phases in the presence of interactions and consistently signals their breakdown at the onset of a charge density wave (CDW). We further demonstrate that non-Hermiticity enhances interaction effects: While moderate changes occur under periodic boundary conditions, open boundary conditions lead to a pronounced amplification of staggered charge correlations near exceptional points. This enhancement arises from the accumulation of low-energy states near exceptional points, which promotes electronic instabilities and strengthens CDW tendencies.
summary: This paper analyzes the interacting non-Hermitian SSH model to understand how charge ordering competes with topological phases. Using real-space markers and correlation functions, the authors show that interactions enhance charge correlations near exceptional points under open boundary conditions. The findings provide a detailed picture of topology breakdown driven by electronic instabilities.
main_problem: The study investigates the complex interplay between topological order, charge correlations, and interaction effects within a non-Hermitian quantum model.
main_result: Non-Hermiticity enhances interaction effects, leading to pronounced amplification of staggered charge correlations near exceptional points under open boundary conditions. The real-space topological marker remains robust in diagnosing phase transitions despite interactions.
method: The authors employ Exact Diagonalization (ED) and mean-field treatments on the interacting non-Hermitian Su-Schrieffer-Heeger model to map out the phase diagram.
model_or_system: The system is a one-dimensional, interacting non-Hermitian Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) model. The Hamiltonian includes nearest-neighbor hopping terms with non-reciprocity ($\delta$) and an interaction term ($V$).
key_observables: Real-space topological marker ($W$), charge structure factor ($S_{	ext{cdw}}$), many-body energy gaps ($	ext{Re}(E_{	ext{gap}}), 	ext{Im}(E_{	ext{gap}})$), and local density profiles ($\langle n_i angle$).
important_parameters: Interaction strength ($V$), non-reciprocity parameter ($\delta$), boundary conditions (Periodic vs. Open), and proximity to exceptional points.
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on defining the ground state eigenpair using Krylov-Schur diagonalization, and careful consideration is given to eigenvector choices (left vs. right) to avoid artifacts.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate phase diagrams showing $W$, $S_{	ext{cdw}}$, and energy gaps as functions of interaction strength ($V$) and hopping parameters ($\delta$), comparing PBC and OBC results.
paper_structure: The paper systematically analyzes the model using multiple diagnostic tools: first establishing the topological marker's robustness, then mapping out phase transitions (Topo-I $\leftrightarrow$ CDW), and finally detailing how non-Hermiticity enhances correlations near exceptional points under OBC.
why_interesting: This work bridges concepts from topology (non-Hermitian physics) with strong correlation effects (CDW formation) in a 1D lattice model, which is highly relevant for understanding correlated electronic states in synthetic quantum materials or open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Schematic representation of the hoppings in the non-Hermitian dimerized SSH model, see Eq. (1). Phase di- agram of the system for periodic, (b) [open, (c)], boundary conditions. Topo-I and Topo-II stand for the topological Her- mitian and non-Hermitian phases, respectively, with unity or half-unity winding number, and the trivial insulator phase with zero winding number. Phase boundaries are determined by finite-size scaling of charge correlations, many-body energy gaps, and the topological marker. Data are shown for t2 = 1, δ = 0.6, computed from left and right eigenvectors.

FIG. 2. Results in the V vs t1 plane, showing (a) the topolog- ical marker, (b) the charge structure factor, and (c) the real [(d) imaginary] many-body energy gap. Topo-I and Topo- II stand for the two topological phases with W = 1 and W = 1/2, respectively, and the trivial insulator phase pos- sesses W = 0. Data for δ = 0.6, and N = 24. Here, SηR cdw(π) is computed with left and right eigenvectors (i.e., η = L).

FIG. 3. The CDW structure factor at (a) q = π under PBC and (b) at q = π−2π/N under OBC. (c) Finite-size-collapsed CDW structure factor under PBC; the critical interaction Vc = 5.4 ± 0.1 is obtained by minimizing the collapse cost function (inset). (d) CDW correlation ratio (see text) as a function of V under OBC, where the dashed line marks the crossing point Vc ≈5.2 for different system sizes. Data are for t1 = 1.6, and δ = 0.6, obtained using left and right eigen- vectors.

FIG. 4. Charge structure factor as a function of δ for several lattice sizes for V = 8, and t1 = 0.5 (left panels), and t1 = 1.5 (right panels); (a) and (b) present data for PBC, while (c) and (d) display data for OBC, all for q = π. The insets in panels (c) highlight the enhanced region. Panels (e) and (f) display results for OBC this time for q = π −2π/N.
FIG. 5. Local density under OBC for L = 24, V = 8, t1 = 0.5, for different values of δ. All data computed using the left-right (LR) convention.
